cognitive_score.notes: Fluctuating and environmentally dependent. Stress or unfamiliar settings reduce capacity.

caa_severity.details: Out of control bleeds noted in Queensland Health imaging from September 2022. Cerebral amyloid plaque deposits present.

capacity_framework.dependency: Environmentally dependent (calm environments improve capacity, stress worsens it)
capacity_framework.support_level: Graded (can perform tasks with specific supports)
capacity_framework.decision_making_model: Supported decision-making required, not blanket removal of rights

key_dates.description: Queensland Health imaging confirms CAA and 20/30 cognitive score.
Andrew sends case details to a lawyer, seeking assistance.
Dr. Harvey from Nundah CCDT conducts an assessment.
Dr. Harvey issues a letter that omits the assisted capacity framework.
Bank of Queensland (Bec O'Connell) demands Cheryl's removal from the SMSF.
BOQ rejects all of Andrew's attempts at compliance regarding the SMSF.
Cheryl is admitted to Royal Brisbane and Women's Hospital (RBWH).
Zachary Hutchins (RBWH social worker) threatens to initiate QCAT proceedings.
QCAT hearing is held without Cheryl's notification.
The Public Trustee of Queensland is appointed as administrator.
Andrew discovers that Cheryl was never notified of the QCAT hearing.
ADA Australia (Leitzel Kidd) becomes involved in the case.
Ryan's Rule is invoked due to concerns about Cheryl's care.
Andrew briefs BlueCare on the details of the QCAT scandal.
Urgent re-referral made to Dr. Harvey; no appointment has been scheduled for 10 months.

failures: Sent Cheryl to a property with a mold issue in August 2023, leading to a mastocytosis infection.
Demanded private payment for ensuring her safety.
Accumulated $9,000 in arrears.
Accepted a $9,000 payment in January 2024 but never resumed services, effectively disappearing for over two years.
Actions described as "horrifying" by ADA Australia.
Presented Dr. Harvey's letter at the QCAT hearing out of context, framing it as "no capacity" which was a misrepresentation.
Failed to involve key personnel like Dr. Harvey, Cheree Ambrose, Suzi, and Judy in the assessment.
Did not conduct a specialist assessment at the STARS facility.
Coordinated with Bec O'Connell of Bank of Queensland during the SMSF dispute, exacerbating the crisis.
His actions led to Cheryl's rights being stripped without her knowledge or representation.
In November 2023, demanded Cheryl be removed from her SMSF when a term deposit was maturing.
Rejected all of Andrew's legitimate attempts to comply with their demands.
Prolonged the crisis through the Christmas and New Year holiday period.
Coordinated with Zachary Hutchins to apply pressure on Andrew and Cheryl.
Contributed to the creation of "evidence" that was used against Andrew in the QCAT hearing.
Appointed in February 2024 based on a flawed QCAT decision.
Conducted only one in-person visit over a 24-month period.
Depleted Cheryl's bank account to "very limited funds."
Removed Andrew's access to the CommBank portal, which severely hampered insurance coordination.
Changed the contact email for Centrelink to their own, isolating Cheryl from her financial information.
Resisted providing an ATM card for Cheryl's use.
Demanded the dissolution of the SMSF.
Acted as a guardian, which was beyond the scope of their appointment as an administrator (ultra vires).
Delayed necessary property repairs for four years.
Failed to discover black mold in the property until December 2025.
Responded to multiple Internal Dispute Resolution (IDR) complaints with generic, unhelpful statements.
Applied pressure to close claims prematurely.
Closed both of Cheryl's claims on February 4, 2026, despite outstanding issues and objections.

achievements: Successfully regained possession of Cheryl"s house after a four-year battle.
Enabled Cheryl to return home after multiple hospitalizations.
Maintained the SMSF despite significant external pressure to dissolve it.
Protected Cheryl"s dignity and rights throughout the ordeal.
Coordinated complex insurance, property, medical, and legal matters.
Paid a $9,000 bill to Home Instead, who then failed to provide services.
Self-funded legal costs amounting to thousands of dollars.
Engaged ADA Australia for advocacy support.
Currently preparing a Section 61 appeal to challenge the QCAT decision.

immediate_actions.task: File a formal complaint with Metro North Health regarding Zachary Hutchins.
Send a formal email to Daniel at the Public Trustee of Queensland to clarify their position on the SMSF and information access.
Follow up with Dr. Harvey"s office regarding the 10-month wait for an appointment.

weekly_actions.task: Request the official QCAT decision for case G52248 from the Public Trustee or QCAT.
File a complaint with the Aged Care Quality and Safety Commission regarding Home Instead.
File a complaint with the Queensland Ombudsman regarding the Public Trustee.
File complaints with the Australian Financial Complaints Authority (AFCA) against Bank of Queensland and Comminsure, as the IDR process has been exhausted.

monthly_actions.task: Secure legal representation from an elder law solicitor, possibly through a referral from ADA Law.
Draft a Section 61 appeal to formally challenge the QCAT decision.
Draft a Section 63 application to protect Cheryl from the actions of the Public Trustee.
Compile and organize all evidence for legal proceedings.
Brief the BlueCare team on the case developments.

evidence_files.description: Email from Andrew explaining the QCAT scandal, dated August 28, 2025.
Official certificate proving that Cheryl was never notified of the QCAT hearing.
Current ACAT assessment that documents Cheryl"s fluctuating capacity.
Email chain between Bec O"Connell and Zachary Hutchins showing coordinated pressure.
Documentation of the $9,000 payment to Home Instead and their subsequent failure to provide services.
Bank statements and records showing the depletion of Cheryl"s account by the Public Trustee.
Email from "Evie" that demonstrates incompetence or unprofessional conduct.
Correspondence from ADA Australia expressing their shock at the handling of the case.
The original Queensland Health imaging report from September 2022, confirming a 20/30 cognitive score and CAA.
A summary or analysis of over 500 emails related to the case.
